title: Installed plugin file_path only resolves home dir; project-scope entries orphan
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

**Dimension**: Functional Correctness / Testability Coverage

**Location**: src/plugins/install.rs:173-182 (file_path)

**Violation**: InstalledPlugins::file_path always returns ~/.openclaudia/plugins/installed_plugins.json regardless of InstallScope (Managed, User, Project, Local). Yet PluginInstallEntry carries scope + project_path distinguishing per-project installs. A user with multiple projects, each having their own plugin installs, has ALL scopes writing to the same global file. Consequences:
- Two different projects installing the same plugin name at different paths trigger the upsert match-by-scope-and-project_path logic (install.rs:157-164) correctly — BUT if one project is deleted, the entry remains forever (no cleanup).
- If dirs::home_dir() returns None (unusual, but possible in restricted environments), it falls back to RELATIVE path .openclaudia/plugins/installed_plugins.json — this then changes depending on the process CWD. A long-running agent changing cwd between sessions writes to DIFFERENT files, fragmenting install state.
- No project-scoped file path even though scope enumerates Project.

**Mandated Refactor**:
1. Split into two tracking files: global ~/.openclaudia/plugins/installed_plugins.json (User/Managed scopes) and .openclaudia/plugins/installed_plugins.json (Project/Local scopes).
2. InstalledPlugins::load should merge them; save should dispatch each entry to the correct file based on scope.
3. If dirs::home_dir() returns None, return an error (not a relative-path fallback) — fail fast.
4. Add a `prune_stale()` method that drops entries whose install_path no longer exists.
